title: Default command doesn't detect already-running proxy on second run
description: start_daemon writes Popen wrapper PID to proxy.pid, but with start_new_session=True the actual uvicorn child gets a different PID. On second run, _proxy_is_running() reads the dead wrapper PID, thinks proxy is down, starts a second one. Port conflict or orphaned process.

title: Node.js MCP package for npx install (worthless-mcp)
description: CLAUDE.md says MCP Server is Node.js. Need thin npm package so Claude Code/Cursor auto-install via npx. Pattern: npx worthless-mcp calls uvx worthless mcp under the hood. Unlocks .mcp.json auto-discovery.

title: Python audit remediation backlog from 2026-04-08 review
description:
Consolidated follow-up from .planning/research/python-review/, especially 2026-04-08-ranked-remediation-plan.md.

Purpose:
- track the concrete code and test fixes surfaced by the deep Python audit
- prioritize resource-control, correctness, and boundary-cleanup work
- avoid losing audit findings across ad hoc fixes

Primary references:
- .planning/research/python-review/2026-04-08-ranked-remediation-plan.md
- .planning/research/python-review/2026-04-08-pass-1.md
- .planning/research/python-review/2026-04-08-pass-4-tests-and-gaps.md

title: Native Windows proxy startup path is internally inconsistent
description:
Current Windows proxy startup logic writes the Fernet key to child stdin, but proxy config still only reads from fd or environment variables. The path is weakly tested and currently inconsistent.

Required outcomes:
- either complete and test the stdin-based key transport path
- or fail fast and clearly for unsupported native Windows proxy lifecycle commands
- keep V1 behavior aligned with the Windows support decision and existing hardening tickets

Related existing issues:
- worthless-9op
- worthless-lxq
- worthless-uc2

Primary references:
- .planning/research/python-review/2026-04-08-ranked-remediation-plan.md
- src/worthless/cli/process.py
- src/worthless/cli/commands/up.py
- src/worthless/proxy/config.py
- tests/test_config.py
notes: Mirrored to Linear WOR-231 (child of WOR-230)

title: Keyring roundtrip integration test (store then read, no mocks)
description: All current keyring tests mock one side. Need a test that stores via store_fernet_key with real keyring, reads via read_fernet_key, and verifies the value survives encode/decode. Mark as @integration, skip on headless CI.

title: Key rotation test: delete + re-enroll proves old shards invalidated
description: delete_fernet_key is tested in isolation but no test verifies the full rotation flow: delete keyring entry, re-enroll, verify new key is different, verify old encrypted shards fail to decrypt.

title: Empty WORTHLESS_FERNET_KEY env var silently falls through cascade
description: read_fernet_key checks 'if env_val:' — empty string is falsy and falls through to keyring/file without warning. Could confuse users who set the var but left it blank. Should warn or error on empty.

title: SR-01: fernet key read_bytes() returns non-zeroable bytes
description: bootstrap.py:50 reads fernet key via Path.read_bytes() which returns bytes (immutable, non-zeroable). Should use os.open+os.read into bytearray, or read via fd. The key stays in memory as an uncontrolled bytes object.

title: CLI UX: human-readable errors with --debug for tracebacks
description: Errors should show human message + error code + 'run with --debug for details'. No tracebacks unless --debug. Progressive disclosure pattern.

title: CLI UX: first-run welcome and quickstart hint
description: When no keys are enrolled, worthless status shows empty table with no guidance. Detect first-run, print 3-line quickstart: create .env, run worthless lock, run worthless wrap.

title: Windows experimental support — fix up/down/wrap on Windows
description: Epic for Windows-specific bugs blocking experimental support. P1 startup path fix is required, P3 hardening (ACLs, signals, Job Objects) is best-effort for v1.1.

title: SKILL.md + platform bridge files for agent discovery
description: V1 scope includes SKILL.md but no ticket exists. Create: (1) SKILL.md at repo root for Claude Code, (2) .cursor/rules/worthless.mdc for Cursor, (3) .windsurfrules entry for Windsurf, (4) skill.json for OpenClaw/ClawHub. Draft and cross-platform research at docs/research/skill-md-research.md. Depends on stable CLI surface (worthless-0bf).

title: P2: Shard_a directory symlink injection — no symlink check
description:
shard_a_dir files have no symlink check. Attacker with same-user access could place symlink at ~/.worthless/shard_a/<alias> pointing to controlled file, causing proxy to read attacker-supplied shard_a bytes.
Fix: add is_symlink() check in shard read path, matching the existing .env symlink check in lock.py.
Linear: Harden milestone
Source: security-auditor lifecycle analysis #3
notes: Threat model reference: docs/research/threat-model.md (T-C4 Shard A extraction)

title: P1: Foreground proxy.wait() has no timeout — zombie hangs CLI forever
description:
WHY: In _start_foreground, proxy.wait() blocks with no timeout. If the proxy process becomes a zombie or hangs on I/O, the CLI hangs forever with no way to recover except kill -9.

WHAT:
1. Replace bare proxy.wait() with a poll loop (e.g., proxy.poll() every 1s with overall timeout)
2. After timeout, log a warning and attempt SIGTERM -> SIGKILL escalation
3. Surface a clear error message to the user rather than silent hang

HOW TO VERIFY:
- Unit test: mock a hanging subprocess, confirm CLI exits within timeout
- Manual: start proxy with a sleep-forever stub, confirm CLI recovers

FILES: src/worthless/cli/commands/up.py (_start_foreground), src/worthless/cli/process.py
RELATED: WOR-172

title: CLI and MCP need a stable internal service boundary
description:
The audit found glue-layer drift rather than one isolated bug:

- MCP imports private CLI helpers from status.py
- async MCP tools call blocking sync helpers
- adapter relay logic depends on a private httpx attribute
- status and console behavior bypass the intended abstraction layers

Required outcomes:
- internal status / scan service helpers are callable by both CLI and MCP
- MCP stops reaching through command-private functions
- adapter relay logic stops depending on private httpx internals
- status/error reporting contracts are clearer and less exception-swallowing

Primary references:
- .planning/research/python-review/2026-04-08-ranked-remediation-plan.md
- src/worthless/mcp/server.py
- src/worthless/cli/commands/status.py
- src/worthless/cli/console.py
- src/worthless/adapters/types.py

title: Test suite should enforce intended security contracts, not current loopholes
description:
The Python audit found several cases where tests codify current implementation quirks instead of the intended contract.

Examples:
- request-body tests normalize no-Content-Length pass-through
- Windows platform tests cover helper shape more than end-to-end lifecycle coherence
- the newer Windows Fernet transport path lacks meaningful coverage

Required outcomes:
- replace tests that normalize weak behavior
- add coverage for Windows proxy-path support decisions
- add concurrency tests where in-memory state mutates across awaits

Primary references:
- .planning/research/python-review/2026-04-08-ranked-remediation-plan.md
- .planning/research/python-review/2026-04-08-pass-4-tests-and-gaps.md
- tests/test_proxy_hardening.py
- tests/test_platform.py
- tests/test_config.py
- tests/test_rules.py
notes: Threat model reference: docs/research/threat-model.md (Appendix A verification methods). Competitive reference: docs/research/competitive-landscape.md (security claims need contract tests)

title: PID file hardening: flock, O_NOFOLLOW, process identity
description:
State machine analysis found architectural gaps in PID file handling:
1. No file locking — concurrent down/down or down/up can race on PID file
2. No O_NOFOLLOW — symlink attack on PID file enables arbitrary process kill
3. No process identity verification — PID reuse allows killing unrelated process (amplified by killpg targeting entire process group)

Fix: flock PID file during read/write/signal operations, use O_NOFOLLOW on open, store process start time or cmdline hash in PID file for identity verification before signaling.

Sources: chaos-engineer #4/#12, security-auditor #1/#2/#3, qa-expert #17/#33/#34

title: Move SAST workflow to nightly + main-merge only
description:
WHY: SAST checks (Semgrep, Bandit, Gitleaks, Actionlint, Zizmor) run on every push and PR, consuming ~50% of GitHub Actions minutes. These catch security patterns that rarely change between pushes — running them on every commit wastes the 2000 min/month free-tier budget.

WHAT:
1. Change SAST workflow trigger from push/PR to: nightly cron schedule + on merge to main
2. Keep pre-commit hooks (Gitleaks) running locally on every commit
3. Optionally add a manual workflow_dispatch trigger for on-demand runs

HOW TO VERIFY:
- SAST workflow does not trigger on feature branch pushes or PRs
- SAST workflow triggers nightly and on merge to main
- GitHub Actions minutes usage drops ~50%

FILES: .github/workflows/sast.yml (or equivalent security workflow file)

title: Compliance & Privacy Foundation (SOC2, GDPR, CCPA)
description: Establish foundational compliance guardrails and data privacy controls. This epic covers the technical requirements for SOC2, GDPR, and CCPA compliance, including IP pseudonymization, audit logging for administrative actions, and data retention policies.

title: Add free-tier live tests (Gemini + OpenRouter)
description: Current live tests rely on paid OpenAI (429 quota) and cheap Anthropic. Add Gemini (free 15rpm) and/or OpenRouter (free models) so the full attack suite runs without spending money. Gemini uses a different API format — needs a new _post_gemini helper and adapter.

title: SR-01: unlock shard_a read_bytes() creates intermediate non-zeroable bytes
description: unlock.py:43 does bytearray(shard_a_path.read_bytes()) — the inner read_bytes() creates a bytes object that can't be zeroed before the bytearray copy. Should use os.open+os.read directly into bytearray.

title: CLI UX: color output with NO_COLOR support and icons for accessibility
description: Green for success, red for denied, dim for metadata. Respect NO_COLOR env var (clig.dev standard). Pair every color with an icon for color-blind users.

title: GSD: onboard Phase 13 — Security Hardening (WOR-150)
description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 13. Linear: WOR-150. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

title: GSD: onboard Phase 12 — Migration (WOR-157)
description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 12. Linear: WOR-157. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

title: GSD: onboard Phase 11 — Python Layer Rewire (WOR-148)
description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 11. Linear: WOR-148. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

title: GSD: onboard Phase 10 — Distribution (WOR-149)
description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 10. Linear: WOR-149. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

title: GSD: onboard Phase 9 — Sidecar Hardening (WOR-155)
description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 9. Linear: WOR-155. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

title: GSD: onboard Phase 8 — Sidecar Core (WOR-147)
description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 8. Linear: WOR-147. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 7. Linear: WOR-146. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

title: GSD: onboard Phase 6 — Shamir Core (WOR-145)
description: Run /gsd:discuss-phase and /gsd:plan-phase for Phase 6. Linear: WOR-145. Must go through GSD milestone workflow before execution.

title: Validate landing page claims against shipped V1 CLI
description: Landing page copy (docs/research/landing-page-copy.md) promises specific commands (enroll, wrap, scan, status, daemon) and UX flows (90-second setup, pre-commit hook). Before publishing, verify every claim against the actual shipped CLI surface. Flag any commands that don't exist yet or behave differently than described.

title: Per-request auth token for proxy-to-reconstruction channel
description: Threat model Rec #3: the proxy-to-reconstruction internal channel relies on network isolation (Docker network) but has no per-request authentication. A container escape or misconfigured network exposes Shard B in transit. Add short-lived per-request HMAC token so reconstruction service rejects unauthorized callers. Research: docs/research/threat-model.md Recommendation #3.

title: Distroless runtime image + shell removal
description: Switch Dockerfile runtime stage from python:3.12-slim to gcr.io/distroless/python3-debian12 or similar. Removes shell, package manager, reduces attack surface. Requires: (1) rewrite entrypoint.sh as a Python script since no sh available, (2) replace CMD shell form with exec form, (3) healthcheck must use a compiled binary or be removed in favor of orchestrator probes. Research doc: docs/research/docker-hardening-research.md item #5/#6. Blocked on deciding whether docker exec debugging story is acceptable without shell.

title: P2: Alias inference allows zero-knowledge local proxy abuse
description:
WHY: Any local process on 127.0.0.1 can reach the proxy. Combined with alias inference (_infer_alias_from_path), an attacker who only knows the provider name (e.g., 'openai') can make requests without knowing the enrolled alias — the proxy resolves it automatically when only one key is enrolled per provider. No request signing or mTLS exists in local mode.

WHAT:
1. For V1: document this as a known limitation in threat model (local-only attack, requires same-user access)
2. For Harden: add optional request signing (HMAC header) or localhost mTLS so only the CLI/sidecar can reach the proxy
3. Consider removing alias inference or requiring explicit alias in requests

HOW TO VERIFY:
- V1: threat model doc mentions local proxy access risk
- Harden: unsigned requests to proxy are rejected when signing is enabled

FILES: src/worthless/proxy/app.py (_infer_alias_from_path), SECURITY_RULES.md (threat model)
MILESTONE: Harden

title: Windows: os.open mode bits ignored — NTFS ACL enforcement for PID/log/DB files
description:
os.open(path, flags, 0o600) mode parameter is silently ignored on Windows. Files inherit parent directory ACLs which are typically user-private on NTFS, but not guaranteed.

For V1: acceptable — NTFS defaults are user-private.
For Harden: use win32security to set explicit DACL restricting to current user SID on:
- proxy.pid (process.py write_pid)
- proxy.log (up.py _start_daemon)
- worthless.db (bootstrap.py)
- fernet.key (bootstrap.py)

Source: Windows platform expert review of WOR-172. Item 6.
Milestone: Harden

title: Windows: SIGINT not delivered to DETACHED_PROCESS — need named pipe/event shutdown
description:
signal.SIGINT is never delivered to a process started with DETACHED_PROCESS (no console). signal.SIGTERM maps to TerminateProcess (hard kill, no handler).

Current V1 workaround: TerminateProcess (acceptable per threat model). For graceful shutdown that allows key zeroing (SR-02):
- Option A: Named pipe — child watches for write, triggers graceful shutdown
- Option B: Named event (win32event.CreateEvent) — child watches with WaitForSingleObject
- Option C: SetConsoleCtrlHandler with CTRL_BREAK_EVENT (requires console)
- Option D: Sentinel file — child polls for existence

Recommendation: Named event is cleanest. Child creates event at startup, parent signals it for shutdown.

Source: Windows platform expert + platform engineer reviews of WOR-172.
Affects: down.py, up.py _start_daemon, process.py forward_signals
Milestone: Harden

title: Windows: taskkill /T unreliable for DETACHED_PROCESS — use Job Objects or TerminateProcess
description:
taskkill /T (tree kill) does not reliably kill processes started with DETACHED_PROCESS because Windows job objects are not automatically assigned. The child may survive.

Fix: Create a Job Object with JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE and assign the child to it at spawn time. Alternatively, use ctypes TerminateProcess as primary kill method, taskkill /T as fallback.

Source: Windows platform expert review of WOR-172 plan. Item 3 in windows-support analysis.
Affects: down.py _kill_tree(), process.py forward_signals()
Milestone: Harden

title: worthless down: structured --json output for all 8 scenarios
description:
WHY: Agents and CI scripts parse structured output, not human messages. 'worthless down --json' must return consistent machine-readable output for all 8 exit scenarios (clean stop, force kill, not running, stale PID, permission error, timeout, already stopping, unknown error).

WHAT:
1. Define a JSON schema: {action, pid, port, error_code, message}
2. Ensure all 8 code paths in down.py emit this schema
3. Add error_code enum to docs or CLI help

HOW TO VERIFY:
- Each scenario returns valid JSON with all 5 fields
- error_code is never null (use 'none' for success)
- Test: pytest parametrize over all 8 scenarios with --json flag

FILES: src/worthless/cli/commands/down.py, tests/test_cli_down.py
notes: Part of 'down' command improvement cluster. Implement before worthless-i3u (port verify depends on JSON schema). Related: worthless-7wq (--force), worthless-5sl (exotic OS).

title: worthless down: verify port is free before exit
description:
WHY: Without port verification, 'worthless down && worthless up' can race — down exits 0 but the port is still in TIME_WAIT, causing up to fail with EADDRINUSE.

WHAT:
1. After stopping the proxy process, poll the port (e.g., try connect to 127.0.0.1:PORT) with backoff up to 5s
2. If port is still bound after timeout, exit with a warning (not error — process is dead, port is OS-level)
3. Alternative: add retry-with-backoff to 'up' command instead

HOW TO VERIFY:
- 'worthless down && worthless up' succeeds reliably (no EADDRINUSE)
- 'worthless down --json' includes port_released: true/false

FILES: src/worthless/cli/commands/down.py, tests/test_cli_down.py
DEPENDS: worthless-btz (--json output schema)
notes: Depends on worthless-btz for --json schema. Part of the 'down' command improvement cluster: worthless-7wq (--force), worthless-btz (--json), worthless-i3u (port verify), worthless-5sl (exotic OS).

title: worthless down --force flag (skip SIGTERM, immediate SIGKILL)
description:
WHY: CI/CD scripts and agents should not wait 5s for graceful SIGTERM during 'worthless down'. Pipeline duration multiplies across jobs when each waits for graceful shutdown.

WHAT:
1. Add --force/-f flag to 'worthless down' that sends SIGKILL immediately (skip SIGTERM + grace period)
2. Update --json output to reflect action=force-killed vs action=stopped
3. Document in CLI help text

HOW TO VERIFY:
- 'worthless down --force' kills proxy immediately (< 1s)
- 'worthless down --force --json' returns {"action": "force-killed", ...}
- '--help' shows --force flag

FILES: src/worthless/cli/commands/down.py, tests/test_cli_down.py
notes: Part of 'down' command improvement cluster: worthless-btz (--json output), worthless-i3u (port verify), worthless-5sl (exotic OS scenarios). Implement together for coherent UX.

title: Adversarial test suite — pytest marker + baseline coverage
description:
Two-tier adversarial test strategy:

V1 (internal, colocated): Add @pytest.mark.adversarial to existing race/permission/corrupt-input tests in test_cli_down.py, test_cli_up.py, etc. These stay near the functional tests they relate to. Register marker in pyproject.toml.

Harden (external, dedicated files): test_adversarial_proxy.py, test_adversarial_crypto.py etc. Hypothesis fuzz for PID file contents, proxy HTTP smuggling, signal injection, timing attacks on crypto paths. Separate files because adversarial surface is large.

Both tiers use the same marker — pytest -m adversarial runs everything. CI gate: nightly job.
notes: Threat model reference: docs/research/threat-model.md (defines attack surfaces to test). Competitive reference: docs/research/competitive-landscape.md (differentiation claims need test proof)

title: Docker live e2e test with real LLM API
description:
WHY: Current Docker e2e tests use mocked LLM responses. A live test with a real API key validates the full proxy chain including TLS, DNS, provider auth, and token metering against a real upstream.

WHAT:
1. Add a test_e2e_docker_live.py that builds the Docker image, starts the container, enrolls a real API key, sends a chat completion, and asserts a valid response
2. Gate behind pytest marker (e.g., @pytest.mark.live) and environment variable (WORTHLESS_LIVE_API_KEY)
3. Requires Docker-in-Docker or a dedicated CI runner with Docker access

HOW TO VERIFY:
- WORTHLESS_LIVE_API_KEY=sk-... pytest -m live tests/test_e2e_docker_live.py passes
- Test cleans up container and enrolled key after run

FILES: tests/test_e2e_docker_live.py (new), tests/test_e2e_live.py (reference pattern), Dockerfile
DEPENDS: Working Docker image (already exists)

title: Create THREAT_MODEL.md
description: Standalone threat model doc. Trust boundaries, what's in scope, what's not. Referenced from README security section. Can be based on existing docs/security-model.md.
notes: Deferred to v1.1 — important but not launch-blocking

title: Purchase worthless.sh domain
description: Buy the worthless.sh domain. Wire up curl worthless.sh | sh installer script. Update install-solo.md.

title: Evaluate TestSprite before next sprint (WOR-58)
notes: Deferred to v1.1 — tooling evaluation, not product

title: Audit log for administrative enrollment actions
description: Create an audit_log table in SQLite to track all config changes (who, what, when) triggered via the CLI. Required for SOC2.
notes: Deferred to v1.1 — compliance, not launch-blocking

title: Pseudonymize client IPs (Rate Limiter)
description: Hash client IPs used in the Rate Limiter memory tracking instead of storing raw IP strings. Helps fulfill GDPR/CCPA PII requirements.
notes: Deferred to v1.1 — GDPR, not day-1

title: MPC: eliminate runtime key assembly. Current XOR reconstruct materializes key in memory during upstream call. MPC would compute provider auth without ever assembling the full key. Kills 'single point of compromise' (brutus point 1) and makes 'Zero Standing Keys' fully honest (brutus point 4). Also tighten marketing claim to 'no standing keys at rest' until MPC ships. Defer to Harden milestone.
notes: Deferred to v2 Harden milestone — MPC eliminates runtime key assembly

title: Read-only /data volume — split enrollment from proxy
description: Mount /data as :ro on the proxy container so a compromised proxy cannot modify the shard database. Enrollment would run as a separate init container or one-shot command that mounts /data as :rw. Changes the deployment model. Research doc: docs/research/docker-hardening-research.md item #12.

title: cosign image signing for published container images
description:
WHY: Without image signing, users cannot verify that a pulled container image was built by the Worthless CI pipeline and not tampered with in transit or at the registry.

WHAT:
1. Add cosign sign step to the Docker publish workflow using keyless mode (OIDC from GitHub Actions)
2. Document verification command in README: cosign verify ghcr.io/worthless/...
3. Only matters once images are published to a registry (GHCR or Docker Hub)

HOW TO VERIFY:
- 'cosign verify --certificate-identity=... --certificate-oidc-issuer=... ghcr.io/worthless/proxy:latest' succeeds
- CI logs show cosign sign step completing

FILES: .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml (or equivalent), README.md (verification docs)
DEPENDS: Publishing to a container registry (not yet in V1)
